messages.content: A member fell during class at Iron Gate Athletics. They signed our waiver — how exposed am I on liability if they pursue a claim?
Chris needs careful language on liability at Iron Gate; legal-awareness should flag limits without playing attorney.

Chris, Iron Gate Athletics should treat any injury report as both an operational and a legal triage moment. A waiver can matter for assumption of risk, but enforceability varies by state facts and drafting — I am not your lawyer. What I can say as a business lens: document the incident timeline, preserve sign-in and waiver records, and loop a licensed attorney early if a demand letter appears. Meanwhile, review whether your waiver language matches how classes are actually coached; gaps there are where claims get traction even when a form was signed.
